prompt: Same env as before — all five libpq env vars are exported and point at the live PGDATABASE=evaldb fixture, PG_DOCKER_ARGS=--network=host is set. There's already an `./.claude/skills/pg-evaldb/` directory from a previous (now-stale) generation: it has a leftover sentinel file `references/_stale_marker` and the old `references/tables.md` ends with a line `STALE-FROM-PREVIOUS-RUN`. Run /postgres-skill-creator and overwrite it. Schemas drift, the skill is supposed to wipe and rewrite — confirm it actually does.

assertions.text: ./.claude/skills/pg-evaldb/references/_stale_marker no longer exists — the directory was wiped on regeneration, not patched in place
./.claude/skills/pg-evaldb/references/tables.md does NOT contain the substring `STALE-FROM-PREVIOUS-RUN` — the file was rewritten from introspection, not appended to
./.claude/skills/pg-evaldb/SKILL.md exists and is non-empty after regeneration
./.claude/skills/pg-evaldb/README.md exists and has no unsubstituted `<...>` placeholders after regeneration

./.claude/skills/pg-evaldb/references/tables.md exists, is non-empty, and contains all five seeded table headings (`public.users`, `public.products`, `public.orders`, `public.order_items`, `analytics.events`)
./.claude/skills/pg-evaldb/references/enums.md exists and lists the four `order_status` labels
./.claude/skills/pg-evaldb/scripts/query.sh exists, is executable, and `bash query.sh "SELECT count(*) FROM users"` against the fixture returns `3`
